That\u2019s where your **revenue model** comes in. It\u2019s the blueprint that defines how your business earns income\u2014from subscriptions and product sales to commissions and ads. Whether you\u2019re launching a startup, refining an existing business, or just exploring ideas, understanding different revenue models can help you make smarter strategic decisions. In this article, we\u2019ll break down the most common types of revenue models, show real-world examples of companies using each one, and help you decide which approach fits your business best. Contents *   [What Is a Revenue Model?](https://businessmodelanalyst.com/revenue-models-types/#What_Is_a_Revenue_Model) *   [10 Revenue Models Types](https://businessmodelanalyst.com/revenue-models-types/#10_Revenue_Models_Types)     *   [Advertising Revenue Model](https://businessmodelanalyst.com/revenue-models-types/#Advertising_Revenue_Model)     *   [Subscription Revenue Model](https://businessmodelanalyst.com/revenue-models-types/#Subscription_Revenue_Model)     *   [Freemium Revenue Model](https://businessmodelanalyst.com/revenue-models-types/#Freemium_Revenue_Model)     *   [Transaction/Commission-Based Revenue Model](https://businessmodelanalyst.com/revenue-models-types/#TransactionCommission-Based_Revenue_Model)     *   [Product Sales (Direct Sales) Revenue Model](https://businessmodelanalyst.com/revenue-models-types/#Product_Sales_Direct_Sales_Revenue_Model)     *   [Licensing Revenue Model](https://businessmodelanalyst.com/revenue-models-types/#Licensing_Revenue_Model)     *   [Affiliate Revenue Model](https://businessmodelanalyst.com/revenue-models-types/#Affiliate_Revenue_Model)     *   [Data Monetization Revenue Model](https://businessmodelanalyst.com/revenue-models-types/#Data_Monetization_Revenue_Model)     *   [Pay-Per-Use Revenue Model](https://businessmodelanalyst.com/revenue-models-types/#Pay-Per-Use_Revenue_Model) *   [How to Choose the Right Revenue Model](https://businessmodelanalyst.com/revenue-models-types/#How_to_Choose_the_Right_Revenue_Model)     *   [1. Whether you\u2019re launching a startup, refining an existing business, or just exploring ideas, under", "relevance_score": 0.8}, {"title": "Top Marketplace Business Models To Generate Revenue", "url": "https://multimerch.com/blog/marketplace-business-models-revenue/", "snippet": "[Jina Extracted Content] Title: Top Marketplace Business Models To Generate Revenue URL Source: https://multimerch.com/blog/marketplace-business-models-revenue/ Markdown Content: ![Image 1: marketplace-business-models-revenue-banner](https://multimerch.com/images/marketplace-business-models-revenue-banner900x300.png) _Note: We've updated this article in April 2023!_ In the rapidly growing world of online marketplaces, creating a sustainable and profitable business model is crucial for long-term success. With an abundance of options and strategies to choose from, finding the perfect fit for your marketplace platform can be challenging. In this comprehensive guide, we'll look at various marketplace revenue models, examine their advantages and disadvantages, and offer tips and insights to help you make the most informed decision for your platform. _This article is part of our series on [starting an online marketplace business](https://multimerch.com/blog/start-online-marketplace-business-guide/). If you need additional information related to this article, please contact us._ Here are the marketplace revenue models we'll be looking at: *   [Commission or Selling Fees](https://multimerch.com/blog/marketplace-business-models-revenue/#selling-fees) *   [Subscriptions](https://multimerch.com/blog/marketplace-business-models-revenue/#recurring-payments-subscriptions) *   [Listing or Publishing Fees](https://multimerch.com/blog/marketplace-business-models-revenue/#listing-publishing-fees) *   [Payment Processing or Transaction Fees](https://multimerch.com/blog/marketplace-business-models-revenue/#payment-processing-fees) *   [Sign-up Fees](https://multimerch.com/blog/marketplace-business-models-revenue/#signup-fees) *   [Sponsored Listings and Promotions](https://multimerch.com/blog/marketplace-business-models-revenue/#sponsored-listings) *   [Advertising](https://multimerch.com/blog/marketplace-business-models-revenue/#ads) *   [Auxiliary Fees and Other Models](https://multimerch.com/blog/marketplace-business-models-revenue/#auxiliary-fees) *   [Affiliates and Referrals](https://multimerch.com/blog/marketplace-business-models-revenue/#affiliates-referrals) *   [Bonus: designing a marketplace membership system](https://multimerch.com/blog/marketplace-business-models-revenue/#design-membership-system) *   [Conclusion: which revenue model is right for you?](https://multimerch.com/blog/marketplace-business-models-revenue/#conclusion-best-business-model) Let's jump right in: ## **Commission or Selling Fees** Commission fees, also known as selling or sales fees, are among the most popular revenue models for online stores and marketplaces. You'll see it used on the vast majority of platforms - from the Amazons and eBays of the world down to the smaller local and niche marketplaces. And for a reason. Implementing this revenue model can be challenging, but when done correctly, it can become a reliable and scalable source of income. When you run a marketplace with commission fees, you earn", "relevance_score": 0.8}, {"title": "Marketplace Revenue Models for 2025 (Full Breakdown)", "url": "https://www.shipturtle.com/blog/exploring-revenue-models-for-marketplaces-in-2025", "snippet": "[DDG Snippet] Types ofRevenueModelsIn Detail:a)Commission-BasedModelIn thismodel, youtakeapercentage ofeachsale made by a vendor on your marketplace. For example, if a vendor sells an item for $100 and yourcommissionrate is10%, you earn $10from that sale. Thismodelworks best for platforms that are looking to scale quickly and have lots oftransactions. It's great for marketplaces with ...", "relevance_score": 0.9}, {"title": "Channel Partner Commission Structure: A Full Guide To Fair RevShare ...", "url": "https://www.scaleo.io/blog/channel-partner-commission-structure-a-full-guide-to-fair-revshare-compensation/", "snippet": "[Jina Extracted Content] Title: Channel Partner Commission Structure: A Full Guide To Fair RevShare Compensation By Scaleo URL Source: https://www.scaleo.io/blog/channel-partner-commission-structure-a-full-guide-to-fair-revshare-compensation/ Published Time: 2026-03-10T09:11:35+00:00 Markdown Content: Channel partner programs are one of the fastest ways to add distribution without adding headcount. But the engine lives or dies on how you pay partners. Commission design determines who joins, how hard they sell, and whether your unit economics hold up. Below is a 2026-ready, operator-grade playbook to build a fair, scalable, and compliant partner commission structure\u2014complete with examples, tables, and practical guardrails you can implement today. [![Image 1: partner marketing software for igaming industry](https://www.scaleo.io/blog/wp-content/uploads/2023/01/scaleo-partner-marketing-software-developed-fori-igaming-industry-scaled.jpg)](https://www.scaleo.io/igaming) ### **What is a channel partner commission structure?** A channel partner commission structure is the operating model behind how referral partners, resellers, agencies, and alliance partners get paid. In practice, that means more than a percentage. It includes the commission basis, attribution rules, renewal ownership, clawback windows, payment cadence, and the reporting view partners use to validate earnings. If those elements are vague, the program looks generous on paper and chaotic in production. ## Channel Partner Commission Structure: A Practical Blueprint Use the table below as a working template. It\u2019s intentionally modular so you can map it to reseller, referral, agency, and technology alliance motions without rewriting your entire plan. | **Tier** | **Partner Type** | **Base Commission** | **Requirements** | **Pay Cadence** | **Stackable Incentives** | | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | | **Level 1** | Registered / Entry | 5\u201310% of eligible revenue | No minimums; agree to T&Cs | Monthly | Marketing toolkits, basic co-branded assets | | **Level 2** | Certified | 10\u201315% | $10k/quarter closed-won + basic certification | Monthly | Priority support, solution training | | **Level 3** | Silver | 15\u201320% | $25k/quarter + advanced cert; deal registration use | Bi-monthly | Lead sharing, sandbox access, beta features | | **Level 4** | Gold | 20\u201325% | $50k/quarter + QBRs; pipeline hygiene SLAs | Bi-monthly | Dedicated CAM, MDF (co-op) funds |\n...\nTitle: Channel Partner Commission Structure: A Full Guide To Fair RevShare Compensation By Scaleo URL Source: https://www.scaleo.io/blog/channel-partner-commission-structure-a-full-guide-to-fair-revshare-compensation/ Published Time: 2026-03-10T09:11:35+00:00 Markdown Content: Channel partner programs are one of the fastest ways to add distribution without adding headcount. But the engine lives or dies on how you pay partners. Commission design determines who joins, how hard they sell, and whether your unit economics hold up. Below is a 2026-ready, operator-grade pla", "relevance_score": 0.8}]
